title: santolina chamaecyparissus subvar. heterophylla
intro: Santolina chamaecyparissus subvar. heterophylla, commonly known as White Lavender, is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which have been used to treat various health issues such as digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions. The health benefits of White Lavender include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation, making it a popular herbal remedy for stress relief and anxiety. White Lavender can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its essential oil can be used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
